# TinadecCore — Module Knowledge Base

**Generated:** 2026-07-15
**Generated By:** CatPaw (Core skeleton rebuild)
**Last Updated:** 2026-07-16
**Last Updated By:** Codex (Core storage foundation)
**Last Verified Commit:** (current working tree)
**Branch:** core'try

## AI MAINTENANCE PROTOCOL
- Treat this file as living project memory.
- When changing module boundaries, dependencies, MAF versions, or API contracts, update this file in the same change.
- Verify rules against source files before relying on them.

## OVERVIEW
TinadecCore is a .NET 10 modular monolith built on Microsoft Agent Framework (MAF) 1.13.0.
It is the sole state authority for TinadecOffice: sessions, runs, tasks, approvals, events, traces, model routes, and tool policy.

## STRUCTURE
```
TinadecCore/
├── Directory.Build.props          # Central MSBuild properties (net10.0, nullable, implicit usings)
├── Directory.Packages.props       # Central package version management (MAF 1.13.0, no floating)
├── TinadecCore.slnx               # Sub-solution: foundation / modules / host + tests
├── Contracts/                     # Pure C# DTOs — no MAF/ASP.NET/F# deps
├── Abstractions/                  # Port interfaces + ITinadecCoreBuilder + ModuleDescriptor
├── Persistence/                   # Shared DB abstraction, paths, migration coordination
├── Storage.Migrations.Sqlite/     # SQLite migrations for Memory + Lifecycle contexts
├── Storage.Migrations.PostgreSql/ # PostgreSQL migrations for Memory + Lifecycle contexts
├── Strategies/                    # F# pure strategy kernels (budget, selection, scoring, loop, state)
├── DmaEA/                         # Dual-layer multi-agent collaboration (Planning + Execution)
├── Models/                        # Provider instances, routing, readiness
├── Context/                       # ContextPack with evidence, sources, token budget
├── Prompts/                       # Deterministic prompt assembly
├── Memory/                        # Session serialization, chat history, retention
├── Skills/                        # AgentSkillsProvider, SKILL.md, SEP-2640
├── LoopGuard/                     # Loop detection, iteration limits, budget checks
├── Lifecycle/                     # Run/task/agent/tool/approval state and audit events
├── Runtime/                       # Full composition: AddTinadecCore() / AddTinadecCoreMinimal()
├── Api/                           # ASP.NET Core minimal API (health, manifest, readiness, Gateway stubs)
└── tests/
    ├── TinadecCore.Architecture.Tests/   # NetArchTest boundary enforcement
    ├── TinadecCore.AgentFramework.Tests/ # MAF smoke + F# interop + trimming
    └── TinadecCore.Api.Tests/            # WebApplicationFactory endpoint tests
```

Solution folders (`TinadecCore.slnx`):
- `/src/foundation/` — Contracts, Abstractions, Persistence, Strategies
- `/src/modules/` — eight business modules
- `/src/host/` — Runtime, Api
- `/tests/` — Architecture, AgentFramework, Api tests


## DEPENDENCY GRAPH
```
Contracts (no deps)
   ↑
Abstractions (Contracts + MAF.Abstractions + AI.Abstractions)
   ↑
Persistence (EF Core + SQLite + Npgsql EF; no business modules)
   ↑
Strategies F# (Abstractions)
   ↑
8 business modules (Abstractions + Strategies + MAF as needed; later Persistence for DbContext)
   ↑
Runtime (Persistence + all 8 modules + Abstractions)
   ↑
Api (Runtime, Web SDK)
```

- Business modules do NOT reference each other directly. They collaborate through Abstractions ports.
- Persistence is shared infrastructure, not a harness business module (not counted in the 8-module manifest).
- Only the Api project uses `Microsoft.NET.Sdk.Web`.
- MAF types must not appear in HTTP DTOs or EventEnvelope.

## PERSISTENCE (DATABASE ABSTRACTION)
- Default provider: **SQLite** local file (`TinadecPersistence:Sqlite:DatabasePath`, default `data/tinadec.db`). No Docker required.
- Optional provider: **PostgreSQL** via `TinadecPersistence:Provider=PostgreSql` + `ConnectionStrings:TinadecCore`.
- Modules register EF Core DbContexts with `options.UseTinadecDatabase(sp)` / `ITinadecDatabaseConfigurer` — business code stays provider-agnostic and uses LINQ.
- `AddTinadecPersistence(configuration, contentRootPath)` registers options, connection info, configurer, Core-owned data paths, migration coordination, and readiness probe. It does not own business entities.
- `MemoryDbContext` owns `projects` and `sessions`; `LifecycleDbContext` owns `runs` and `event_index`. Shared mappings use only provider-neutral CLR types and string-backed state values.
- SQLite uses `Storage.Migrations.Sqlite` and migrates automatically at local startup. PostgreSQL uses `Storage.Migrations.PostgreSql` and migration startup is opt-in through `TinadecPersistence:ApplyMigrationsOnStartup`.
- `.github/workflows/core-storage-postgres.yml` provisions PostgreSQL 16 and runs the provider-specific migration/storage contract test.
- Session histories atomically replace files under `data/sessions/`; task snapshots, immutable JSONL events, and artifacts live under `data/tasks/`, `data/events/`, and `data/artifacts/`.
- `GET /api/v1/readiness` includes `storage: { provider, state, detail }` from `IDatabaseReadiness` (`SELECT 1` probe).

## MAF VERSIONS (locked in Directory.Packages.props)
- `Microsoft.Agents.AI.Abstractions` 1.13.0
- `Microsoft.Agents.AI` 1.13.0
- `Microsoft.Agents.AI.Workflows` 1.13.0
- `Microsoft.Agents.AI.OpenAI` 1.13.0
- No preview/alpha packages. No Anthropic provider (only preview available as of 2026-07-15).

## MODULE REGISTRATION
- Each module implements `IModuleRegistrar` with an explicit `Register(ITinadecCoreBuilder)` method.
- `Runtime/TinadecCoreServiceCollectionExtensions.cs` provides:
  - `AddTinadecCore()` — registers all 8 modules (full composition).
  - `AddTinadecCoreMinimal()` — registers only DmaEA, Models, Lifecycle (trimming proof).
- No reflection scanning. Each `AddTinadec...()` extension is explicit.

## API ENDPOINTS (port 48731)

### Core endpoints (fully implemented)
| Endpoint | Method | Description |
|----------|--------|-------------|
| `/api/v1/health` | GET | Legacy `{name, status, version, time}` structure. |
| `/api/v1/harness/manifest` | GET | Returns dual-layer Agent, MAF version, 8 module descriptors, framework info. |
| `/api/v1/readiness` | GET | MAF assemblies loadable = ready; unconfigured modules use `warning` + `not_configured`. |
| `/api/v1/projects` | GET/POST | Lists and creates projects with a canonical unique root path. |
| `/api/v1/sessions` | GET/POST | Lists and creates session metadata. |
| `/api/v1/sessions/{id}` | PATCH | Updates the session title. |
| `/api/v1/sessions/{id}/messages` | GET/POST | Reads/writes versioned Core-owned history files. |
| `/api/v1/sessions/{id}/runs` | GET | Lists durable lifecycle run metadata. |
| `/api/v1/events` | GET SSE | Replays indexed JSONL events, optionally by session and sequence. |

### Gateway proxy stubs (`Api/Endpoints/StubEndpoints.cs`)
All Gateway-proxied endpoints are mapped. GET endpoints return 200 with empty collections/default values. Write endpoints return 501 Not Implemented. This allows the Gateway BFF to compose responses (e.g. `model-center/overview`, `agent-center/overview`) and the Desktop frontend to load without crashes.

Stub groups:
- **Readiness**: `doctor`, `model-readiness`, `model-catalog-readiness`, `tool-layer-readiness`
- **Projects/Sessions**: `orchestration`, `tool-executions`, `task-nodes`, `context-packs`, `supervision-findings`, `invoke-stream`; project/session/message/run/event routes are implemented above.
- **Tools**: `tools`, `tools/search`, `tools/shell`, `runs/{runId}/tools/{toolId}/execute`
- **Approvals**: `approvals` (GET/POST), `approvals/{id}/decision`
- **Model**: `model-provider-templates`, `model-providers` (CRUD), `model-routes`, `model-settings` (GET/PUT) — required by Gateway BFF composition
- **Prompts**: `prompt-fragments` (CRUD), `prompt-context/preview`, versions, effectiveness, signals, compare
- **Agents**: `agents`, `agent-modes`, `agent-candidates`, agent CRUD, `agent-evolution/*`
- **Market/Extensions**: `market/sources`, `market/catalog`, `extensions/*`
- **MCP/ACP**: `mcp/servers`, `acp/adapters`
- **Debug**: `debug/traces`, `debug/spans`, `debug/metrics`, `debug/snapshot`, `debug/diagnostics`, `debug/processes`, `debug/simulate/*`, `debug/breakpoints`

All JSON output uses `snake_case` via `JsonNamingPolicy.SnakeCaseLower`.

## F# INTEROP RULES
- F# modules compile to static classes in C#.
- Use `using TinadecCore.Strategies;` then `LoopDetection.detectRepeatCalls(...)`.
- F# public functions return only C#-compatible types: `int[]`, `bool`, `double`, `(string, float)[]`.
- No F# DU, list, option, async, or curried function in public signatures.

## CONVENTIONS
- `snake_case` JSON at the API boundary; C# properties use PascalCase.
- `EventEnvelope` uses schema version 1.0 and snake_case. No MAF/F# types in payload.
- `ModuleDescriptor.RegistrationStatus`: `Registered`, `NotConfigured`, `Disabled`.
- Gateway transparently proxies all Core endpoints; no Gateway changes needed for stub endpoints.

## COMMANDS
```powershell
# Restore
Remove-Item Env:Version -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
Remove-Item Env:Ice-Version -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
dotnet restore TinadecCore/TinadecCore.slnx

# Build
dotnet build TinadecCore/TinadecCore.slnx --no-restore

# Test
dotnet test TinadecCore/TinadecCore.slnx --no-build

# Run API
dotnet run --project TinadecCore/Api/TinadecCore.Api.csproj --urls http://127.0.0.1:48731
```

## CURRENT STATE (skeleton + Gateway stubs)
- Source projects include Persistence, two provider-specific storage migration projects, and 13 prior projects; 3 test projects.
- Modules are skeleton implementations: interfaces registered, no real business logic.
- Shared storage is registered with four relational index tables and Core-owned file stores. Startup reconciles indexed events from valid JSONL records and reports an incomplete final JSONL line as a storage diagnostic.
- Gateway-proxied endpoints outside the storage surface remain stubs: GET returns empty/default responses and writes return `501`.
- Gateway BFF composition (`model-center/overview`, `agent-center/overview`) works end-to-end with Core stubs.
- Desktop frontend can load without connection errors when Gateway proxies to Core.
- No business schemas beyond storage, no real provider calls, no vector store, and no full dual-layer runtime.
- `loop_guard` and `lifecycle` are the only modules with `Registered` status; all others are `NotConfigured`.
- `Microsoft.OpenApi` 2.0.0 has a known vulnerability warning (transitive from `Microsoft.AspNetCore.OpenApi`); not blocking for skeleton.

## NOT IMPLEMENTED THIS ROUND
- Additional business schemas beyond the storage foundation, model/provider configuration, and approval configuration
- Real model provider calls (IChatClient with actual HTTP)
- Vector store selection
- Full dual-layer agent runtime (DmaEA orchestration flow)
- Tool execution and approval gates
- Real business logic behind stub endpoints (currently return empty/501)
